name: Birth of Lucerys Velaryon

proposed.name: Birth of Lucerys Velaryon
proposed.identity_line: Princess Rhaenyra gives birth to her second son, Lucerys, in 115 AC.

edges.quote: Princess Rhaenyra had given birth to a second son late in the year 115 AC.

notes: Quote verified at line 33. Principals: Rhaenyra Targaryen (mother). Character node rhaenyra-targaryen exists.

name: Birth of Joffrey Velaryon

proposed.name: Birth of Joffrey Velaryon
proposed.identity_line: Princess Rhaenyra gives birth to her third son, Joffrey, on Dragonstone in 117 AC.

edges.quote: In 117 AC, on Dragonstone, Princess Rhaenyra bore yet another son.

notes: Quote verified at line 45. Principals: Rhaenyra Targaryen. Joffrey named after Ser Joffrey Lonmouth.

name: Secret marriage of Rhaenyra and Daemon

proposed.name: Secret Marriage of Rhaenyra and Daemon
proposed.identity_line: Princess Rhaenyra secretly marries her uncle Prince Daemon Targaryen on Dragonstone in 120 AC.

edges.quote: The marriage had been performed on Dragonstone, suddenly and secretly.
The marriage had been performed on Dragonstone, suddenly and secretly.

notes: Quote verified at line 131. This marriage occurs after the death of Laenor Velaryon and Laena Velaryon. Daemon already husband to Laena before she died.

name: Wedding of Aegon the Elder and Helaena

proposed.name: Wedding of Aegon II and Helaena Targaryen
proposed.identity_line: King Viserys weds his son Aegon the Elder to his daughter Helaena in 122 AC.

edges.quote: Following the ancient tradition of House Targaryen, King Viserys wed his son Aegon the Elder to his daughter Helaena.
King Viserys wed his son Aegon the Elder to his daughter Helaena.
King Viserys wed his son Aegon the Elder to his daughter Helaena.

notes: Quote verified at line 145. Aegon was 15, Helaena was 13 at marriage. This is a Targaryen custom marriage (incestuous).

name: Fall of the Dragonstone garrison

proposed.name: Fall of the Dragonstone Garrison
proposed.identity_line: Castle servants rose against Aegon II's garrison, yielding survivors to Alyn Velaryon

edges.quote: delivering the rest in chains to young Alyn Velaryon

notes: Quote verified at line 61. Alyn Velaryon received the captured prisoners. This event results from internal mutiny, not direct dragon/knight assault. | S203 orchestrator fix: 131 AC post-Dance holdout; Daemon edge was a hallucination (dead); Alyn harm-gate fix VICTIM_IN->PARTICIPATES_IN.

name: Marriage of Lyonel Hightower and Samantha Tarly

proposed.name: Marriage of Lyonel Hightower and Samantha Tarly
proposed.identity_line: Young Lyonel Hightower finally takes Samantha Tarly as his wife after years as paramours.

edges.quote: and finally taking her as his wife when a new High Septon came to power in the Starry Sept
and finally taking her as his wife when a new High Septon came to power in the Starry Sept

notes: Quote verified at line 169. Prior High Septon had forbidden the marriage on grounds of incest; new High Septon reversed the ruling. Samantha was widow of Lyonel's father Lord Ormund.

name: Massacre at Cobbler's Square

proposed.name: Massacre at Cobbler's Square
proposed.identity_line: The gold cloaks, overwhelmed by the Shepherd's mob, are slaughtered at Cobbler's Square.

edges.quote: Battle turned to riot turned to slaughter.
Battle turned to riot turned to slaughter.

notes: Quote verified at line 79. The Shepherd's followers rise up against the City Watch. Ser Luthor Largent dies in this battle, crushed by a cobblestone.

name: Yielding of the Red Keep

proposed.name: Yielding of the Red Keep
proposed.identity_line: Ser Garth the Harelip yielded the Red Keep to Perkin the Flea

edges.quote: Ser Garth chose instead to strike Rhaenyra's banners, open his gates, and trust to the mercy of the foe
Ser Perkin the Flea and his gutter knights appeared outside the gates, demanding that the castle yield

notes: S203 orchestrator override: quote found at line 249 end-of-paragraph; agent missed it.

name: Trystane mounts the Iron Throne

proposed.name: Trystane Mounts the Iron Throne
proposed.identity_line: Ser Perkin the Flea's squire Trystane sits upon the Iron Throne as a mummer king during the Moon of Madness.

edges.quote: Ser Perkin's gangling squire Trystane mounted the Iron Throne.
Ser Perkin's gangling squire Trystane mounted the Iron Throne.

notes: Quote verified at line 259. Trystane is one of three false kings who rule during the chaos after Rhaenyra's flight. He is later beheaded by Ser Alfred Broome.

name: Poisoning of Ulf White

proposed.name: Poisoning of Ulf White
proposed.identity_line: Ser Hobert Hightower poisons Ulf White using poisoned wine during their negotiations.

edges.quote: his wine cup was deadlier than Valyrian steel
his wine cup was deadlier than Valyrian steel.

notes: Quote verified at line 147. Ulf White was one of the Two Betrayers (dragonriders who switched sides). Ser Hobert also dies from the poison, making it a mutual poisoning.

name: Death of Queen Rhaenyra

proposed.name: Death of Queen Rhaenyra
proposed.identity_line: Queen Rhaenyra is devoured by King Aegon's dragon Sunfyre on Dragonstone in 130 AC.

edges.quote: the golden dragon devoured the queen in six bites.
Septon Eustace tells us that the golden dragon devoured the queen in six bites, leaving only her left leg below the shin 'for the Stranger.'

notes: Quote verified at line 305. Rhaenyra is fed to Sunfyre by order of King Aegon II as vengeance for the death of his sister Helaena and her children. She was 33 years old, had reigned as queen for half a year.

name: Return of Aegon II to King's Landing

proposed.name: Return of Aegon II to King's Landing
proposed.identity_line: King Aegon II returns to King's Landing from Dragonstone in the waning days of 130 AC.

edges.quote: In the waning days of 130 AC, King Aegon II returned at last to King's Landing.

notes: Quote verified at line 89. Aegon travels via ship escorted by Velaryon war galleys. He arrives in a closed litter as he cannot walk due to his injuries.

name: Deaths of the Two Toms

proposed.name: Deaths of the Two Toms
proposed.identity_line: Tom Tangletongue is executed and his father Tom Tanglebeard is drowned during Larys Strong's coup.

edges.quote: Tom Tangletongue was surprised in the castle yards as he was leaving the stables, and beheaded forthwith.

His father Tom Tanglebeard was absent from the castle, but they found him in a tavern on Eel Alley. When he protested that he was 'just a simple fisherman, come to have an ale,' his captors drowned him in a cask of same.

notes: Quote verified at line 255 (father). Two fishermen/supporters of King Aegon II who were eliminated during the coup that put Prince Aegon the Younger on the throne. Ser Alfred Broome executed Tom Tangletongue.

name: Stillbirth and death of Visenya

proposed.name: Stillbirth and Death of Visenya Targaryen
proposed.identity_line: Princess Rhaenyra gives premature birth to a stillborn daughter Visenya on Dragonstone in 129 AC.

edges.quote: When the babe at last came forth, she proved indeed a monster: a stillborn girl, twisted and malformed, with a hole in her chest where her heart should have been, and a stubby, scaled tail.

notes: Quote verified at line 189. The child was born prematurely due to Rhaenyra's rage upon hearing news of King Aegon II's coronation. The malformed nature of the stillborn child caused Rhaenyra additional grief.

name: Green council peace embassy to Dragonstone

proposed.name: Green Council Peace Embassy to Dragonstone
proposed.identity_line: Grand Maester Orwyle leads a peace mission from King Aegon II to Queen Rhaenyra on Dragonstone.

edges.quote: So Grand Maester Orwyle was dispatched across Blackwater Bay under a peace banner, leading a retinue that included Ser Arryk Cargyll of the Kingsguard.
dispatched across Blackwater Bay under a peace banner

notes: Quote verified at line 40. Orwyle brings the king's peace terms, but Rhaenyra rejects them and strips Orwyle of his Grand Maester chain, elevating Maester Gerardys instead. This mission fails to achieve peace.

name: Widow Fairs

proposed.name: Widow Fairs
proposed.identity_line: Mass matchmaking of northern soldiers with riverlands widows at multiple locations

edges.quote: more than a thousand northmen accompanied Black Aly and her nephew Lord Benjicot when they returned to the riverlands after the royal wedding

notes: Quote verified at line 23. Multiple locations mentioned (Raventree, Riverrun, Stoney Sept, Twins, Fairmarket). Lady Alysanne Targaryen credited as originator.

name: Wedding of Aegon III and Jaehaera

proposed.name: Wedding of Aegon III and Jaehaera
proposed.identity_line: Wedding of king Aegon III and princess Jaehaera at Visenya's Hill, 131 AC

edges.quote: the wedding of Prince Aegon and Princess Jaehaera was celebrated out of doors, at the top of Visenya's Hill
the wedding of Prince Aegon and Princess Jaehaera was celebrated out of doors, at the top of Visenya's Hill

notes: Quote verified at line 51. Solemnized on seventh day of seventh moon 131 AC. High Septon of Oldtown performed rites. Aegon crowned afterward with yellow gold circlet.

name: Velaryon succession attack on Alyn

proposed.name: Velaryon Succession Attack on Alyn
proposed.identity_line: Failed assassination plot against Lord Alyn by rival Velaryon claimants

edges.quote: However, the plot to murder their young lord went awry when the guards at Castle Driftmark proved loyal to the Sea Snake's memory and his chosen heir
Ser Malentine was slain during the attempt

notes: Quote verified at line 167. The unnamed Velaryon silent cousins (Daemion and Daeron also tried). Ser Malentine slain, his brother captured and condemned to death; brother (Ser Rhogar) took the black instead.

name: Death of Alicent Hightower

proposed.name: Death of Alicent Hightower
proposed.identity_line: Death of Dowager Queen Alicent from Winter Fever, same night as Lord Westerling

edges.quote: The Dowager Queen Alicent of House Hightower, second wife of King Viserys | and mother to his sons, Aegon, Aemond, and Daeron, and his daughter Helaena, died on the same night as Lord Westerling

notes: Quote verified at line 319. Occurred during 132 AC Winter Fever. Alicent had confined herself to apartments, unraveled mentally ('deep aversion to color green'), spoke of seeing her dead children. Died at hour of the wolf (midnight).

name: Aemond named Prince Regent

proposed.name: Aemond Named Prince Regent
proposed.identity_line: Prince Aemond assumes regency after Aegon II recovers from Meleys burn

edges.quote: named himself only Protector of the Realm and Prince Regent

notes: Quote verified at line 153 with expanded context from line 154. Aemond claimed iron-and-ruby crown but styled himself Protector/Regent, not King. Criston Cole remained Hand.

name: Legitimization of Addam and Alyn

proposed.name: Legitimization of Addam and Alyn
proposed.identity_line: Queen Rhaenyra removes bastardy taint from dragonseed brothers Addam and Alyn of Hull

edges.quote: Addam of Hull, dragonseed and bastard, became Addam Velaryon
Addam of Hull, dragonseed and bastard, became Addam Velaryon

notes: Quote spans lines 235-237. Petition originated from Lord Corlys and was seconded by Prince Jacaerys. Addam thereafter styled heir to Driftmark.

name: Taming of Sheepstealer by Nettles

proposed.name: Taming of Sheepstealer by Nettles
proposed.identity_line: Nettles becomes rider of wild dragon Sheepstealer via patient daily sheep offerings

edges.quote: the brown dragon was brought to heel by the cunning and persistence of a "small brown girl" of six-and-ten

notes: Quote verified at line 249. Nettles was 16 years old, delivered fresh sheep every morning until Sheepstealer accepted her. Multiple accounts of her birth (Munkun=bastard of unknown origin; Mushroom=dockside whore's daughter).

name: Citadel sends white ravens

proposed.name: Citadel Sends White Ravens (130 AC)
proposed.identity_line: Citadel announces approaching winter via three hundred white ravens sent on Maiden's Day

edges.quote: On Maiden's Day in the year 130 AC, the Citadel of Oldtown sent forth three hundred white ravens to herald the coming of winter

notes: Quote verified at line 149-150 in fab-the-red-dragon-and-the-gold-17-p03. Despite the warning of winter, conditions favored Rhaenyra's side militarily (Kingslanders disaffected, Velaryon fleet strong, Vale loyal, North sending aid).

name: Rising of the Shepherd

proposed.name: Rising of the Shepherd
proposed.identity_line: One-handed itinerant preacher stirs masses in Cobbler's Square with anti-dragon sermons during Rhaenyra's reign

edges.quote: there rose up in Cobbler's Square a certain itinerant brother, a barefoot scarecrow of a man ina hair shirt and roughspun breeches, filthy and unwashed and smelling of the sty

notes: Quote verified at line 123 in fab-the-red-dragon-and-the-gold-17-p04. Shepherd was likely a Poor Fellow, had lost right hand, preached dragons as Valyrian demons. Crowd grew to thousands; gold cloaks driven off attempting to suppress.

name: Gunthor slays Alester Wynch

proposed.name: Gunthor Slays Alester Wynch
proposed.identity_line: Gunthor Goodbrother kills commander Alester Wynch in dispute over Lysa Farman

edges.quote: the latter slew the former in a quarrel over Lord Farman's daughter Lysa
the latter slew the former in a quarrel over Lord Farman's daughter Lysa

notes: Quote verified at line 99. Context: Faircastle garrison holding out against Fair Isle rebellion. Lysa was one of Dalton Greyjoy's salt wives. Afterward, longships fled and Fair Isle freed.

name: Birth of Laena Velaryon

proposed.name: Birth of Laena Velaryon (daughter of Alyn)
proposed.identity_line: Baela Targaryen gives birth to daughter Laena while Alyn Velaryon sails to Lys, 134 AC

edges.quote: Baela Velaryon was brought to childbed and delivered of a healthy baby girl

notes: Quote verified at line 139. Occurs while Alyn is away; named after her mother (Alyn's deceased wife). She is daughter of Alyn Velaryon and Baela Targaryen (also known as Baela Velaryon). Node laena-velaryon-daughter-of-alyn already exists in graph. | S203 harm-gate fix: healthy birth is not harm.

name: Ransom pact for Prince Viserys

proposed.name: Ransom Pact for Prince Viserys
proposed.identity_line: Alyn Velaryon agrees to ruinous terms with Lysene magister to ransom missing Prince Viserys

edges.quote: the Iron Throne would pay a ransom of one hundred thousand golden dragons
the Iron Throne would pay a ransom of one hundred thousand golden dragons

notes: Quote verified at line 183. Terms included: 100k dragons ransom, non-aggression vs House Rogare for 100 years, transfer Braavos Bank funds to Rogare Bank, lordships for three Rogare sons, marriage to Larra Rogare permanent. Prince Viserys returned 134 AC.

name: Death of Tyland Lannister

proposed.name: Death of Tyland Lannister
proposed.identity_line: Hand Tyland Lannister dies of Winter Fever in 132 AC, attended by king Aegon III

edges.quote: The fever took his life in two days instead of the usual four

notes: Quote verified at line 15. Died quickly, faster than typical Winter Fever. Aegon III held his hand as he died. Tyland had served both Rhaenyra and Aegon II/III, made no enemies on either side despite blindness and disfigurement from torture.

name: Death of Herman Rollingford

proposed.name: Death of Herman Rollingford
proposed.identity_line: Ser Herman Rollingford slain by Braavosi bravo in duel over doublet color during peace mission

edges.quote: Ser Herman Rollingford was killed in a duel by a bravo who took offense at the color of his doublet

notes: Quote verified at line 215. Occurred during Lord Mooton's peace mission to Braavos following Alyn Velaryon's conflict with the Sealord. Trivial cause of death (doublet color dispute).

name: Death of Aegon II

proposed.name: Death of Aegon II
proposed.identity_line: King Aegon II dies of poison in his litter en route to sept, suspected to be Larys Strong's behest

edges.quote: the king dead upon his cushions
we can have no doubt that it was done at the behest of Larys Strong

notes: Quote verified at line 277-278. Poisoner unknown (Ser Gyles or Ummet food-taster suspected). Aegon had been sickly, ruled only 2 years. Folding row 4 cremation detail: body was carried back to Dragonstone and cremated per Targaryen tradition (no cremation quote provided in input but reference to it in passage context).
